title: Validate Quorum task artifacts on save, not by background sweeps
context: FEAT-004-a had to enforce schemas for blueprint, validation, review, trace, and memory artifacts without expanding runtime scope.
content: Quorum now treats artifact validation as a write-time concern: supported task artifacts are validated immediately before persistence, and invalid payloads are rejected without modifying the existing file. This preserves fast CLI behavior and avoids retroactive scans during task status/start flows.
anti_patterns: Avoided validating every artifact in active/ during status or startup because that adds unrelated runtime work and violates the task's non-goal.
